payload.text: Crée un site internet pour un Collectif au nom de LAMUKA, qui lutte les violence basé sur le genre (VBG) de la femme et jeune fille handicapé, ainsi que la santé sexuelle et reproductive de ces dernières et aussi leur inclusion sociale et leur autonomisation au CONGO Brazzaville. Crée les  différentes pages qu'il faut pour un site complet moderne et dynamique en tenant compte de tout les aspects, expérience utilisateur et responsive pour les écran de téléphone mobile.

**Prioritizing Project Objectives**

I've been focusing on the core purpose: crafting a digital space for LAMUKA that effectively combats GBV, prioritizes reproductive health, and fosters inclusivity. My priority is translating their mission into a modern, responsive website, ensuring a user-friendly and impactful experience for all users, especially on mobile devices. I'm keen on integrating Google Maps as a crucial interactive element.


**Analyzing User Personas & Tech**

My focus has shifted to deeply understanding the target users: women and girls with disabilities, donors, partners, and the general public in the Congo. This has also driven my tech stack decisions, which now features React, TypeScript, Tailwind, and the Gemini API for content generation and the Google Maps tool. The goal is to craft an empowering, accessible, and community-focused experience.


**Mapping Core Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the foundational components: Header, Footer, Hero, ServiceCard, MapComponent (for Gemini's grounding capabilities), and ContactForm.  The planned pages are Home, About, Services, Contact, and Resources. The `geminiService.ts` file will be crucial for the AI integration, potentially driving a Q&A bot and dynamic resource lookup powered by the Google Maps tool. I'm excited by the possibilities here!


**Defining Functional Components**

I'm now focusing on specific components, integrating the `geminiService.ts` file with Gemini-2.5-flash and the Google Maps tool. I'm prioritizing the `ChatAssistant` for map-grounded search, the `Hero` with impactful imagery, and a standard form plus AI map assistant on the `Contact` page. The `Layout` with `Navbar` and `Footer` is also finalized. I will be developing the `Home`, `About`, and `Services` pages, as well.


**Integrating Google Maps Data**

I need to make sure the Gemini API calls use the appropriate tool configuration for Google Maps data integration. I've finalized the file structure, including the AI assistant and Google Maps components, and have a solid mental draft for key components. The Gemini service will be built to integrate with Google Maps, the Assistant component will render map links based on the Gemini response, and Tailwind will style the interface. I'm prioritizing responsive design, clear CTAs, and accessible contrast for polish. Navigation is also finalized with react-router-dom (HashRouter).


**Constructing the Foundation**

I'm now fully immersed in the coding phase, building out the project's skeleton. I've initialized the basic file structure with `metadata.json`, `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `services/api.ts`, `components/Navbar.tsx`, `pages/Home.tsx`, and `pages/Assistant.tsx`.  The `Navbar` is mobile-ready, and I'm currently working on the `Home` page, including the `Hero`, `Stats`, and `Pillars` sections. The `Assistant` page is where the magic (maps!) will happen, using the Gemini service's logic I've started in `api.ts`.

C'est une excellente initiative. Passer à une solution dynamique avec **Supabase** va transformer votre site vitrine en une véritable plateforme de contenu gérable.

Voici la solution complète pour intégrer le CMS (Système de Gestion de Contenu) et les Analytics.

### 📋 Pré-requis : Configuration de Supabase

Avant d'utiliser le code ci-dessous, vous devez exécuter ces commandes SQL dans votre interface **Supabase** (rubrique **SQL Editor**) pour créer la base de données :

```sql
-- 1. Créer la table des articles
create table posts (
  id bigint generated by default as identity primary key,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()) not null,
  title text not null,
  excerpt text,
  content text,
  image_url text,
  category text,
  author text default 'L''équipe LAMUKA',
  read_time text default '5 min',
  views integer default 0
);

-- 2. Activer la sécurité (RLS)
alter table posts enable row level security;

-- 3. Créer une politique : Tout le monde peut lire
create policy "Public Posts are viewable by everyone" 
on posts for select using (true);

-- 4. Créer une politique : Seuls les connectés peuvent modifier/ajouter (Admin)
create policy "Authenticated users can insert posts" 
on posts for insert with check (auth.role() = 'authenticated');

create policy "Authenticated users can update posts" 
on posts for update using (auth.role() = 'authenticated');

create policy "Authenticated users can delete posts" 
on posts for delete using (auth.role() = 'authenticated');

-- 5. Créer un Bucket de stockage pour les images
insert into storage.buckets (id, name, public) values ('blog-images', 'blog-images', true);

-- 6. Politique de stockage (Upload uniquement pour admin, lecture pour tous)
create policy "Public Access" on storage.objects for select using ( bucket_id = 'blog-images' );
create policy "Auth Upload" on storage.objects for insert with check ( bucket_id = 'blog-images' and auth.role() = 'authenticated' );
```

Ensuite, récupérez votre **Project URL** et votre **Anon Key** dans les paramètres de Supabase (Settings > API).

1.  Connectez-vous à votre projet sur [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard).
2.  Dans le menu de gauche, cliquez sur l'icône **Authentication** (celle qui ressemble à deux personnes).

4.  Cliquez sur le bouton vert **Add User** en haut à droite.

    *   **Email** : Votre adresse email.
    *   **Password** : Votre mot de passe.

6.  Cliquez sur **Create User**.

Une fois cela fait, retournez sur votre site, allez sur la page **Administration** (via le lien en bas du site ou en ajoutant `/admin` à l'URL), et connectez-vous avec ces identifiants. Vous aurez alors accès au tableau de bord pour écrire vos articles !
